mnemonics.title: SVD Parts
mnemonics.content: U = Left (U = 'You' - Left side), Σ = Sum/Diagonal values, V = Right (V^T = Transpose of V)

mnemonics.title: Singular Values Order
mnemonics.content: SVD Sorted: Singular Values Definitely Sorted - always Descending (σ₁ ≥ σ₂ ≥ ...)

mnemonics.title: SVD Application Areas
mnemonics.content: SVD Helps: Signal Processing, Data Compression, Image Processing, Statistics (PCA), Solving linear systems

acronyms.usage: Eigenvectors of AA^T, m×m orthogonal matrix

acronyms.expansion: Sigma matrix - diagonal of singular values
acronyms.usage: m×n matrix with non-negative diagonal elements
Eigenvectors of A^TA, n×n orthogonal matrix
Reciprocal of non-zero singular values, transposed

visualTips.visualization: Think of SVD as: First rotate/reflect by V^T, then scale along axes by Σ (stretch/compress), then rotate/reflect by U. Like a 3D transformation: rotate → scale → rotate back.
Imagine a sphere in n-dimensions being transformed to an ellipsoid in m-dimensions. Singular values = lengths of ellipsoid axes. Larger σ = longer axis = more important direction.
If only r singular values are non-zero, the ellipsoid collapses to r-dimensional. Think of a 3D object becoming flat (rank 2) or a point (rank 1).
Keep only top k axes (largest σ). The approximation is like viewing the ellipsoid from far away - you see only the main directions, smaller details blur together.

analogies.analogy: Like breaking a complex machine into its core components: U is the input mechanism, Σ is the power/settings, V is the output mechanism - all essential for the machine to work.
Like reading a book summary - the main plot (large singular values) captures 90% of the story with just 10% of the words (ranks).
Like a photo compression - you keep the main shapes (large σ) and discard fine details (small σ), resulting in smaller file but recognizable image.
Like finding the 'best possible' inverse for singular matrices - similar to finding the closest solution when exact inverse doesn't exist, like finding the nearest exit when main door is blocked.
